title: tagetes epapposa
intro: Tagetes epapposa, a species of marigold, is a plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, characterized by its bright yellow or orange flowers and strong, pungent aroma. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antifungal properties, which make it useful for treating wounds, skin conditions, and infections. The health benefits of Tagetes epapposa include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems, making it a popular herb for natural remedies. Herbal preparations of Tagetes epapposa include teas, infusions, and salves, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
